role: This file is the stylesheet for the 'Source' verb result surface: a token-driven, fixed-position reading card (and its scrim) that displays the fuller record behind a tapped dot.
capabilities: Semi-transparent scrim backdrop with fade-in animation
Fixed-position source card anchored above the V handle on wide viewports
Responsive bottom-sheet layout for narrow viewports
Close button styling with hover and focus-visible states
Verb tag, name, and meaning metadata typography
Body label and pre-wrapped prose content area
Stacked user note cards with distinct ground styling
Empty, status, and error message styling
Token-based theming with zero hardcoded colors
substance_why: The file supplies complete, production-ready styling for every documented surface element, including responsive breakpoints, entrance animations, and full token integration.
interactions_present: Close button hover and focus-visible visual feedback
interactions_missing: No click/tap handler for the close button
No focus trap or focus management
No scroll-lock on the underlying page
performance_risks: Long .v-source-body pre-wrap text can cause expensive reflow on viewport resize
Entrance animations lack a prefers-reduced-motion media query
Fixed overlay with high z-index and semi-transparent scrim can promote large composited layers
No CSS containment on the card or notes list to limit layout/paint scope
reuse_verdict_why: The token-driven architecture and clearly named component seams should be preserved, but the visual form, motion, and interaction wiring will be rebuilt in the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Extract entrance/exit motion rules into a shared animation or motion layer
Split layout/positioning (scrim + card shell) from content typography (tag, name, meaning, body, notes)
Move empty/status/provenance utility styles to a shared status/empty-state stylesheet
Extract the note item component styles to a reusable note-card module
Keep the responsive breakpoint rules co-located with the card shell or move to a dedicated responsive layout layer
